nodes.id: doc:docs/M8-test-matrix.md#M8 — Real-Time Focus + Approvals: 7-type test matrix
nodes.label: M8 — Real-Time Focus + Approvals: 7-type test matrix
merge config env into a launch plan
WHEN applying config env to a launch plan THEN the system SHALL merge the extra env — overriding existing keys and appending new ones, with no duplicates.
WHEN the extra env is empty THEN the launch plan SHALL be unchanged.

WHEN parsing the input the `mcp__regatta__approve` permission tool receives THEN the system SHALL produce `ApprovalRequested{tool, detail}` — detail is the command for a Bash request, else the rendered input.
IF the input lacks a tool name or is malformed THEN the system SHALL return None.
WHEN the dock allows THEN the system SHALL return `{behavior:"allow",updatedInput:<input>}` (null input if malformed).
WHEN the dock denies THEN the system SHALL return `{behavior:"deny",message:<reason>}`.
WHEN an agent's approve request is parsed THEN the dock SHALL surface ApprovalRequested; WHEN the dock allows THEN the agent SHALL receive `{behavior:"allow",updatedInput:<input>}` (proceed).
WHEN the dock denies THEN the agent SHALL receive `{behavior:"deny",message:<reason>}` (stop).
rank sessions that need the human

WHEN scoring a session that is blocked on the human THEN the system SHALL return a priority of 5 for WaitingApproval, 4 for NeedsInput, 3 for Error, and 2 for an unseen Done — so the Attention Dock sorts the most urgent first.
IF a session is actively working or transitional (Starting, Running, Compacting, RateLimited, Paused) THEN the system SHALL return a priority of 0 so it never surfaces in the Attention Dock.
WHEN ordering sessions for the Attention Dock THEN the system SHALL return only the unseen sessions that need the human, most-urgent first (priority descending), preserving input order on ties.
IF every session is working/transitional or already seen THEN the system SHALL return an empty list so the dock shows nothing.
WHEN a session's spend crosses its budget ceiling and the budget action is Block THEN the system SHALL tear the session down (auto-pause).
IF the budget action is not Block THEN the system SHALL NOT pause even when the ceiling is exceeded.
WHEN dispatching by backend THEN `label` and `plan_launch` SHALL route to the right backend (Claude vs Codex).
WHEN parsing a line THEN each backend SHALL parse its own format and return None for the other's.

WHEN planning a fresh Claude session THEN the plan SHALL run `claude` in headless stream-json duplex mode with the chosen model, a forced `--session-id`, the Regatta approval tool (`--permission-prompt-tool`), and a `REGATTA_SESSION_ID` environment tag.
IF resuming an existing session THEN the plan SHALL use `--resume <id>` in place of `--session-id <id>`.
The plan SHALL tag every agent process with a `REGATTA_SESSION_ID` environment variable so the supervisor can identify and reap stray processes — no leaked agents, ever.
WHEN parsing a backend label THEN the system SHALL map "Claude" / "Codex" to a `Backend` (round-tripping with `label()`); an unknown label returns None.
WHEN grouping a session for the board THEN the system SHALL bucket it by how long ago it was last active: Today, Yesterday, Earlier this week, Last two weeks, or Older.
IF the last-activity time is in the future (clock skew) THEN the system SHALL treat it as Today rather than a negative bucket.
WHEN classifying spend against a budget THEN the system SHALL return Ok, Warn (at ≥80% of the limit), or Exceeded (at ≥100%); a non-positive limit is unbounded (Ok).
WHEN deciding auto-pause THEN the system SHALL pause only when spend is Exceeded and the budget's action is Block.
WHEN computing spend rate THEN the system SHALL return USD/hour from total spent and elapsed seconds (zero elapsed → zero rate).
WHEN predicting THEN the system SHALL return the seconds until spend reaches the limit at the current rate — None if spend is not advancing, and zero if already at or over the limit.
WHEN planning a Codex launch THEN the system SHALL produce a `LaunchPlan` with program `codex`, args `exec --json --model <model>`, and the `REGATTA_SESSION_ID` env tag (for reaping).
IF `resume` is true THEN the plan SHALL attach to the rollout via `resume <session_id>`.
WHEN parsing a Codex rollout's `session_meta` line THEN the system SHALL extract the id and cwd into the SAME `SessionMeta` a Claude transcript produces (id, cwd, project label, empty git branch).
IF the line is not a `session_meta`, is malformed, or has no id THEN the system SHALL return None.
parse Codex events into the normalized shape

WHEN parsing a Codex rollout / `codex exec --json` line THEN the system SHALL map `task_started` → SessionStarted, `token_count` → Usage, and an assistant `message` → AssistantText — the SAME `NormalizedEvent` Claude produces.
IF the line is metadata (session_meta / turn_context), a non-assistant or unknown event, or malformed THEN the system SHALL return None.
WHEN config is set in a scope THEN `get_layer(scope)` SHALL return that scope's key→value settings.
IF the same scope + key is set again THEN the value SHALL be updated in place.
WHEN resolving config layers in order (global → project → session) THEN later layers SHALL override earlier keys.
WHEN there are no layers THEN the effective config SHALL be empty.
WHEN cost events are recorded THEN `total_spend`, `spend_by_project` (most-spent first), and `spend_since(ts)` SHALL reflect them.
WHEN no cost events exist THEN the rollups SHALL be zero/empty rather than error.
price token usage and pick the effective cost

WHEN pricing token usage THEN the system SHALL apply per-model rates (Opus 4.8 $5/$25, Sonnet 4.6 $3/$15, Haiku 4.5 $1/$5 per 1M tokens; cache reads at 0.1× the input rate, cache writes at 1.25×; unknown/local models cost $0).
WHEN computing a usage event's cost THEN the system SHALL prefer the authoritative `cost_usd` if present, otherwise price the tokens; a non-usage event costs nothing.
WHEN coalescing a burst of events THEN runs of adjacent AssistantText SHALL merge into one frame, and any other event SHALL break the run and stay a discrete, in-order frame.
WHEN a burst contains an ApprovalRequested THEN it SHALL stay a discrete, in-order frame — never merged or dropped.
WHEN parsing `git diff --numstat` THEN the system SHALL emit per-file added/removed counts; binary files (shown as `-`) yield None counts.
IF a line lacks the three tab-separated fields or has no path THEN the system SHALL skip it.
read a repo's status and diffstat
WHEN reading a git repo THEN `status()` SHALL return its working-tree changes and `diffstat()` the per-file +/- counts (via the pure parsers).
IF the directory is not a git repository THEN `status()` SHALL return empty without error.
WHEN parsing `git status --porcelain` THEN the system SHALL emit one FileChange per file with its status code (M / A / D / R / ?); a rename's path is the new name.
IF a line is blank, too short, or has no path THEN the system SHALL skip it.
a Claude session runs live through the pipeline

WHEN a Claude session is spawned and pumped THEN the system SHALL reflect it live in the registry (model, last text, authoritative cost, turns) and the teardown SHALL reap it — proving both backends run live through one path.

WHEN scanning a directory for transcripts THEN the system SHALL index each `.jsonl` file (reading only its head) into the store's transcript index, skipping files whose first line is not valid session metadata.
IF the directory does not exist THEN the system SHALL return zero without error.
WHEN choosing a layout from a count THEN the system SHALL map 1 → Focus (1 pane), 2 → Split (2 panes), 3+ → Quad (4 panes).
WHEN filling panes THEN the system SHALL assign the first N session ids to the panes, padding empty slots with None.
pump a live session into the registry

WHEN a spawned session's events are pumped THEN the system SHALL apply each `NormalizedEvent` to the live `Registry` as it arrives, so the registry reflects the running session (model, last text, turns, cost).
WHEN summarizing a `SessionRuntime` THEN the system SHALL produce a compact line "model · N turns · $X.XX", using an em dash for an unknown model.
WHEN a key names a secret (contains KEY / TOKEN / SECRET / PASSWORD) THEN `is_secret_key` SHALL be true, and `mask` SHALL hide all but the last 4 characters (values ≤ 4 chars fully masked).
WHEN masking a config THEN secret values SHALL be masked and non-secret values left intact.
WHEN materializing the effective config into session env THEN keys prefixed `env.` SHALL become environment variables, and `local_model_url` SHALL set `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` (the local-model path).
WHEN there is no `local_model_url` THEN no `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` SHALL be set.
Codex is indistinguishable through the pipeline
WHEN a Codex session runs through the supervisor THEN it SHALL produce the same `NormalizedEvent` shape (SessionStarted / AssistantText / Usage) as Claude, and the zero-leak teardown SHALL reap it.
WHEN reattaching THEN the system SHALL replay each persisted session's events from its saved transcript offset and advance the offset, so a later reattach with no new data returns nothing new.
IF a session's transcript file no longer exists THEN the system SHALL skip it without error and still reattach the others.
WHEN applying an event to a session id THEN the registry SHALL create-or-update that id's runtime (a new id is created on first event).
WHEN snapshotting THEN the registry SHALL return every session's (id, runtime), id-sorted.
WHEN resuming a session THEN the system SHALL produce a launch plan that attaches to it via `--resume`, and a copy-paste command `claude --resume <id>` for the board's Copy button.
IF the session id is empty THEN the resume command SHALL still be well-formed (`claude --resume `) and never panic.
summarize a diff for the Review Inbox
WHEN summarizing diff stats THEN the system SHALL sum the added and removed lines across files, counting binary files (no counts) as zero.
WHEN there are no changes THEN the summary SHALL be (0, 0).
WHEN parsing a JSON-RPC line THEN the system SHALL extract id / method / params (id and params default to null); no method or malformed input SHALL return None.
WHEN building a response THEN the system SHALL produce a JSON-RPC 2.0 result or error envelope.
fold the event stream into live state

WHEN folding a `NormalizedEvent` into a `SessionRuntime` THEN the system SHALL set the model on SessionStarted, set the last text + increment the turn count on AssistantText, and accumulate tokens + cost on Usage.
WHEN a Usage event carries no authoritative `cost_usd` THEN its cost SHALL be priced from the tokens by model (Codex/local), via `cost::effective_cost`.
WHEN displaying settings THEN the system SHALL show the effective config (resolved global → project → session) with secret values masked.

WHEN given a session name THEN the system SHALL return a lowercase slug containing only `[a-z0-9-]`, with each run of other characters collapsed to a single hyphen and leading and trailing hyphens removed.
IF the name contains no alphanumeric characters THEN the system SHALL return the literal `session`, never an empty branch name.
WHEN a transcript is upserted into the index THEN the system SHALL persist it and return it from a board search by title or project, ordered most-recent first.
IF the same session id is upserted again THEN the system SHALL update it in place rather than create a duplicate.
WHEN a session is upserted into the store THEN the system SHALL persist it and return it from a list of all sessions ordered by id.
IF a session with the same id is upserted again THEN the system SHALL update it in place rather than create a duplicate.

WHEN a Claude stream-json line of a known type arrives THEN the system SHALL emit the matching normalized event: a `system`/`init` line becomes SessionStarted (with the model), an `assistant` line becomes AssistantText (its text blocks concatenated), and a `result` line becomes Usage (cost and token counts).
IF a line is not valid JSON, has no recognized type, or is an unhandled type THEN the system SHALL return None and never panic.
The parser consumes untrusted process output, so it SHALL never panic and SHALL drop malformed or unrecognized lines (returning `None`) rather than emit a bogus event.
WHEN a session is shut down THEN the supervisor SHALL terminate the entire process group — the agent and any child processes it spawned — leaving no surviving process.
IF shutdown is called on a session whose process has already exited THEN it SHALL complete safely and idempotently, never panicking.
A leaked or runaway agent process is a resource and trust hazard, so teardown SHALL leave no surviving process in the group — no agent ever outlives its session.
tail a transcript from an offset

WHEN tailing a transcript file from a byte offset THEN the system SHALL read only the new complete lines, parse each into normalized events, and return the events plus the next offset — holding a trailing partial (newline-less) line until it completes.
IF the offset is beyond the file's current length (the file was truncated or rotated) THEN the system SHALL restart from the beginning.
WHEN parsing a line of a Claude transcript THEN the system SHALL extract the session id, working directory, git branch, and a project label (the slug of the working directory's last path segment).
IF the line is not valid JSON or has no session id THEN the system SHALL return None.
budget percent and spend by model
WHEN rendering the budget bar THEN `budget_pct` SHALL return the percent of the budget consumed, clamped to 0..100 (a non-positive limit reads as 0%).
WHEN showing the model split THEN `spend_by_model` SHALL group spend by model, most-spent first.
render an event as a display line

WHEN turning a normalized event into a display line THEN the system SHALL map SessionStarted to a `system` line naming the model, AssistantText to a `claude` line carrying its text, and Usage to a `usage` line showing cost and token counts.
IF an assistant event has empty text THEN the line SHALL preserve the empty text rather than drop the turn.
collect normalized events from a session's stdout
WHEN an agent process writes stream-json lines to stdout THEN the supervisor SHALL read them to EOF and return the parsed NormalizedEvents in order.
IF stdout contains blank or unparseable lines THEN the supervisor SHALL skip them, returning only the valid events and never crashing.

WHEN planning a worktree for a session THEN the system SHALL produce a unique branch `regatta/<slug>-<short-uuid>`, an isolated checkout path under the worktrees directory, and the base branch — so sessions on the same repo never collide.
IF the uuid is shorter than 8 characters THEN the system SHALL use the whole uuid and never panic.
